title: List Comprehensions ⚡
description: Create lists in one elegant line.

slides.title: List Comprehensions ⚡
slides.content:
# One-Line List Creation

A **list comprehension** creates a new list from an existing one in a single line:

```
# Traditional way
squares = []
for x in range(5):
    squares.append(x ** 2)

# Comprehension way ⚡
squares = [x ** 2 for x in range(5)]
```

**Syntax:** `[expression for item in iterable]`

Both produce `[0, 1, 4, 9, 16]`, but the comprehension is shorter and more Pythonic! 🐍

slides.title: Comprehensions 🧪
slides.codeSnippet:
# Squares
squares = [x**2 for x in range(6)]
print("Squares:", squares)

# With condition (filter)
evens = [x for x in range(10) if x % 2 == 0]
print("Evens:", evens)

# Transform strings
names = ["alice", "bob", "charlie"]
upper = [n.upper() for n in names]
print("Upper:", upper)

quizData.question: What does [x*2 for x in range(4)] produce?
What does [x for x in range(10) if x > 5] produce?
quizData.explanation: The condition if x > 5 filters to keep only values greater than 5: [6,7,8,9].
